project.objective: Who were the siblings of Charles Robinson Broyles (MJ7L-SL9), born 9 December 1878 and died 18 March 1966, and what evidence supports their relationship to him?

questions.question: What children were enumerated in the household of George Broyles (LCPC-PZK, born abt 1830 Tennessee, residing Marshall, Saline County, Missouri) in the 1880 federal census, and which of those children are siblings of Charles Robinson Broyles (MJ7L-SL9, born 9 December 1878)?
questions.rationale: The 1880 federal census is the pivotal household record coinciding with Charles Robinson Broyles's infancy (age ~1). It will enumerate all children living with his father George Broyles and mother Mary Elizabeth Davis in Marshall, Saline County, Missouri, directly establishing a core roster of full siblings. A source reference (QQK2-QZ8) is already in tree.gedcomx.json but has not yet been extracted into research assertions. This is the gatekeeper question: its answer establishes the baseline sibling list that all downstream searches will corroborate, extend, or qualify. Answering it also clarifies the household at the time of Charles's birth, enabling targeted searches for siblings in subsequent censuses and vital records.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Nine plan items executed across five record types (census, vital records, marriage, probate, obituaries). The 1880 federal census directly and completely answers the core household question. Texas death certificates, SS NUMIDENT, and Missouri marriage records extend and corroborate the sibling roster. Probate and obituary searches returned negative results (no George Broyles probate found, George likely died post-1891 or outside Saline County). One unresolved birth-month conflict (Minnie, c_001) is not material to the sibling identification. All criteria met with acknowledged limitations on derivative-source use.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: YES. The 1880 US Census (src_001) directly enumerates George Broyles's household in Marshall, Saline Co., MO, identifying five co-resident children: Wm. H. Broyles (b.~1858), Geo. W. Broyles (b.~1861), Judy A. Broyles (b.~1868), Minnie Broyles (b.~1877), and Chas. Broyles (b.~1879 = Charles Robinson Broyles MJ7L-SL9). The 1860 census adds two half-siblings from the first marriage: W H Broyles (b.~1858 = same as Wm. H.) and Milton M. Broyles (b.~1859). Texas death certificates add Myrtle Leana Cartmill (b. 17 May 1886) as a post-1880 full sibling. The question is convincingly answered.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: MEETS STANDARD with limitation. FamilySearch searched across census (1860, 1870, 1880, 1900), vital records (Texas Deaths 1890-1977, SS NUMIDENT), Missouri marriage records (collection 2060668), Saline County probate fulltext (imageGroups 007636433 and 007630167), and Missouri obituaries (collection 4373182). 1870 census returned negative (George not in Saline Co. in 1870 — family was in Cooper Co. at that time). Only FamilySearch was searched; physical repositories and Ancestry were not. However, the key records to answer this question were found, making additional repositories unlikely to change the conclusion.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: PARTIAL. Census records (1860, 1880) are indexed derivatives with images available but not directly read via the image-reader tool. Texas death certificates accessed via FamilySearch derivative index entries — original images not verified (specifically, Minnie Bell Strickland's death cert image was not examined, leaving the father's given name unconfirmed from the original). SS NUMIDENT is a derivative of the original SS-5 form. Missouri marriage records are derivative index entries. Original images were available but not accessed for most sources. This is an acknowledged limitation; the consistency of data across multiple independent derivative sources substantially mitigates the risk.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: MEETS STANDARD. At least two independent sources for most sibling identifications: Wm. H. Broyles — 1880 census (son of George) + 1860 census (son of George and Sidney); Judy A. Broyles — 1880 census (daughter of George) + 1886 marriage record naming George Broyles as father; Minnie Broyles — 1880 census + SS NUMIDENT + Texas death cert + 1896 Zumwalt marriage record (four sources). Single-source for Myrtle Leana Cartmill (1970 Texas death cert) and Milton M. Broyles (1860 census only). Geo. W. Broyles confirmed only by 1880 census. These single-source identifications represent a limitation in verification depth.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: MEETS STANDARD. The 1880 and 1860 federal census records are original government documents reflecting contemporaneous enumeration — the strongest available record type for household composition. The Texas death certificates (src_002, src_004) are original state vital records classified as original sources in research.json. Missouri marriage records are official records. At minimum two original records with direct evidence are in the evidence set.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: PARTIAL. One unresolved fact conflict: Minnie Bell Strickland birth month (c_001) — SS NUMIDENT gives 28 Feb 1877; Texas death cert gives 28 Oct 1877. Both sources are secondary; neither can be preferred on quality grounds. The conflict is formally entered and honestly noted per GPS Element 4. Critically, the conflict does not affect the conclusion about q_001: Minnie was born in 1877 regardless of month, making her a full sibling of Charles Robinson Broyles (b. 9 Dec 1878) — both born after George's second marriage to Mary Elizabeth Davis (~1867). The conflict concerns only the month of birth, not the identity or parentage of the individual.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: LOW. The 1880 federal census is an unambiguous primary record enumerating the Broyles household in Marshall, Saline Co., MO. No unsearched record type is likely to name a different roster of children. George Broyles's probate record was sought but not found (likely died after the pre-1891 index coverage, or out of state) — this is the most significant gap, but the sibling roster from multiple concordant sources makes a probate's naming of different heirs unlikely to materially change the conclusion. The most actionable gap is reading the original image of Minnie Bell Strickland's TX death cert (ark:/61903/3:1:33S7-9Y13-3NCM) to obtain the father's given name, which would upgrade the confident link to LCPC-PZK from probable to confident for that one assertion.

items.rationale: Source QQK2-QZ8 in tree.gedcomx.json references the 1880 US Census entry for 'Geo. Broyles and Elizabeth Broyles' in Marshall, Saline, Missouri — the direct household record for Charles Robinson Broyles's father and mother at the time of Charles's birth (Dec 1878). This record is not yet extracted into research assertions. Extracting it will enumerate all children in the household and establish the core roster of siblings. The 1880 Census is 98% name-indexed on FamilySearch (imageGroup 004118864). If the source ARK does not resolve, search collection 6742 (1880 US Census) for George/Geo Broyles in Saline County, MO.

The 1870 Census (3 years after George's second marriage to Mary Elizabeth Davis ~1867) will show any children born between the marriages (1856-1867 gap) or early in the second marriage, who may have grown and left home before the 1880 census. It also documents whether any children from the first wife Liddy/Sidney Shoemaker were still in George's household after remarriage. Saline County 1870 is 97% indexed (imageGroup 004273849_001_M9CN-M42). Search for George Broyles / Geo Broyles in Saline Co., MO.

Source S26H-D5M in tree.gedcomx.json references the 1860 US Census entry for George Broyles and Sidney Broyles in Palestine Township, Cooper County, Missouri — George's household during his first marriage. Children listed there would be half-siblings of Charles Robinson Broyles via his father. Source is already in tree but not extracted into assertions. The 1860 Census is 98% indexed. Extracting this source will identify the half-sibling roster from the first marriage (George + Liddy/Sidney Shoemaker, married 1856).

Extract and search the Missouri County Marriage records (collection 2060668) for Broyles family members marrying in Saline County 1880-1910. Source QYVY-Y9B in tree.gedcomx.json shows 'Joseph S Vandyke and Judie A Broyles' married 1 Sep 1886 — Judie A. Broyles is a strong sibling candidate. Additional Broyles marriage records in this period may identify further siblings. Saline County marriage volumes 1881-1910 are 48-65% indexed and full-text searchable on FS. Search for 'Broyles' as groom/bride surname in Saline Co.

Three tree sources hint at daughters of George Broyles who relocated to Texas: (1) 3SS4-B5C — Texas Deaths 1890-1977 entry for Myrtle Leana Cartmill (d. 16 Jan 1970); (2) 7MDD-6LP — Texas Deaths 1890-1977 entry for Minnie Bell Strickland (d. 5 Nov 1971); (3) QYVY-TNY — SS NUMIDENT for 'Minnie Broyles and George Broyles.' These records are already in tree.gedcomx.json but unextracted. Death certificates name parents; if Myrtle and Minnie name George Broyles and Mary Elizabeth Davis as parents, they are confirmed full siblings of Charles. If they name George Broyles and Sidney/Liddy Shoemaker, they are half-siblings.

If Myrtle Leana Cartmill and Minnie Bell Strickland were daughters of George Broyles who migrated to Texas, the 1900 and 1910 censuses would show their households and confirm birth years and birthplaces consistent with a Saline County, MO origin. Cross-referencing against the 1880 household roster establishes sibling relationships. Search for Myrtle Broyles/Cartmill and Minnie Broyles/Strickland in Texas 1900-1910.

If George Broyles (LCPC-PZK) died in Missouri, his probate record would name his heirs — typically his surviving children — providing a definitive sibling list. Saline County probate volumes for 1876-1907 are on FamilySearch (imageGroups 007636437, 007630169, 007630168, 007630166, 007630167) and are full-text searchable. Also check the general probate index 1821-1891 (imageGroup 007636433). Search fulltext for 'Broyles' in Saline County probate records.

The Missouri Obituaries collection (4373182, 584K records, 1880-1990) may include obituaries for George Broyles or his children listing surviving relatives by name. Obituaries frequently name siblings. Also search the 1881 Saline County local history (imageGroup 007940688, full-text searchable) for a Broyles family biography that may list children. These contextual sources satisfy BCG Standard 14 (topical breadth) and may corroborate or extend the sibling roster established by census records.

The 1900 Census (95-97% indexed, Saline Co., imageGroups 004118864_002_M92X-W2Q and 004118865_001_M9KY-X4M) would show George Broyles's household 20 years after the 1880 census, enumerating any children still at home and listing how many children George's wife had borne and how many were still living. Charles Robinson Broyles (b. 1878) would be ~21 and may be enumerated separately. Search for George Broyles in Saline County 1900 as a corroborating and extending search for siblings not yet identified.

log.notes: Identified George Broyles household (persona ark:/61903/1:1:M6F6-6VT) in Marshall, Saline Co., MO. Wife indexed as 'Elizabeth' (= Mary Elizabeth Davis LCPC-PDH, b. 1851 MO). Household children: Wm. H. Broyles (b. 1858 MO, M6F6-6VB), Geo. W. Broyles (b. 1861 MO, M6F6-6V1), Judy A. Broyles (b. 1868 MO, M6F6-6KM), Minnie Broyles (b. 1877 MO, M6F6-6K9), Chas. Broyles (b. 1879 MO, M6F6-6KS = Charles Robinson Broyles MJ7L-SL9). Core 1880 sibling roster established.
No George Broyles found in Saline County 1870 census. Search returned nil. Widened to statewide Missouri (see next log entry).

Missouri-wide 1870 census search for George Broyles b. ~1830 TN. No confident match — top ranked result was George Broyles born 1850, score ~0.002 (not the subject). George Broyles (LCPC-PZK) may have resided outside Missouri in 1870, or the record is unindexed. Negative finding logged.

Identified George Broyles household (persona ark:/61903/1:1:MHZ5-ZV6) in Palestine Twp, Cooper County, MO — first marriage household with Sidney (Liddy/Sidney Shoemaker b. 1839 MO). Children: W H Broyles (b. 1858 MO, MHZ5-ZVF) and Milton M Broyles (b. 1859 MO, MHZ5-ZVN). These are half-siblings of Charles Robinson Broyles via George's first marriage to Liddy/Sidney Shoemaker.

No match for George Broyles b. ~1830 TN in Saline County 1900 census. One younger George Broyles (b. Sep 1882) appeared in household of Wm Broyles in Arrow Rock Twp — likely a grandson. George Broyles (LCPC-PZK) apparently died before 1900. This eliminates the 1900 household as a sibling source but suggests son William H. Broyles (Wm Broyles) remained in Saline County with a son possibly named George.

42 Broyles/Boyle marriage records returned. High-value candidates: Minnie Broyles married Dee Z Zumwalt (6XWX-JNL3); George Broyles marriage (6XZ4-H9CW); C Broyles (6XSV-31B8); Annie M Broyles + Wm F Fielder (6XSJ-VJ9S); Louisa M Broyles + G L Puckett (6XS2-SDN5); Cana A Broyles + Eugene Williams (6XSR-BWNN). QYVY-Y9B (Judie A Broyles + Joseph Vandyke, Sep 1886) already in tree. Must read individual records to confirm which Broyles women are daughters of George Broyles.

Saline Co. probate general index (007636433, covering 1821-1891) returned 3 hits for Broyles: (1) main index page listing 'Wm Broyles'; (2-3) 1876/1873 probate register pages for a 'Mary Broyles' settlement with Cooper Co. connection. No 'George Broyles' probate entry in the pre-1891 index. George Broyles likely died after 1891 or outside Saline Co. Will also search wills volumes 007630167 (A-B) and 007630166 (C-D 1881-1907).

Top hit: Myrtle Leana Cartmill (KSY2-LWQ), Texas Deaths 1890-1977. Born 17 May 1886, Marshall, Missouri. Died 16 Jan 1970, Elgin, Bastrop, TX. Record names FATHER as George Broyles (KSY2-LW7) and MOTHER as Mary Elizabeth Davis (KSY2-LWW) — direct parentage evidence naming both parents. Confirms full sibling of Charles Robinson Broyles. Original tree source ARK 3SS4-B5C was non-resolvable; correct record ARK is KSY2-LWQ.

Top hit: Minnie Bell Strickland (K36Y-YFJ), Texas Deaths 1890-1977. Born 28 Oct 1877, Missouri. Died 5 Nov 1971, Fort Worth, Tarrant, TX. Widowed. Father listed as 'Broyles' (surname only; given name not captured in index, persona K36Y-YFV). Matches 'Minnie' (b. 1877 MO) in George Broyles 1880 household. Original tree source ARK 7MDD-6LP was non-resolvable; correct record ARK is K36Y-YFJ. Cross-reference: she married Dee Z Zumwalt 14 Apr 1896 (MO marriage record 6XWX-JNL3), later surname Strickland.

6 results in Missouri Obituaries (collection 4373182). Top hits are 'Geo. Boyles' and 'Geo Boyles' from the obituary of 'Grover H Birdie Arthur' — variant spelling, and this is a mention within another person's obituary, not a George Broyles obituary. No direct obituary for George Broyles (LCPC-PZK) found in Missouri Obituaries 1880-1990. George likely died out of Missouri (Mary Elizabeth Davis died in Fort Worth TX 1918, and Charles Robinson Broyles settled in Houston TX), or no obituary was digitized.

query.collection: United States, Social Security Numerical Identification Files (NUMIDENT), 1936-2007

log.notes: SS NUMIDENT for Minnie Broyles Strickland (6K7V-X4BQ). Application Oct 1939. Birth: 28 Feb 1877, Marshall, Saline County, Missouri. Father: George Broyles (6K7V-X4BW). Mother: Elizabeth Davis (6K7V-X4BC). AKA: Minnie Broyles. CONFLICT: birth date 28 Feb 1877 conflicts with TX death cert date 28 Oct 1877 — month discrepancy (Feb vs Oct). Both sources agree on day 28, year 1877, and Marshall, Saline Co. birthplace. This NUMIDENT directly names George Broyles as father and Elizabeth Davis (= Mary Elizabeth Davis) as mother, confirming Minnie Bell Strickland is a full sibling of Charles Robinson Broyles.

Saline County probate Wills volume A-B 1837-1881 (007630167) searched for '+Broyles +George' — 0 results. No George Broyles will found in this volume. Combined with the general index search (log_007) showing only 'Wm Broyles' and 'Mary Broyles' entries pre-1891, there is no George Broyles probate record in Saline County through 1881. George likely died after 1891 (when the general index coverage ends), or out of Saline County.

Minnie applied for Social Security herself in October 1939 — she is the self-informant for her own name. The BirthName field shows 'Strickland / Minnie Broyles', indicating married surname Strickland with maiden name Broyles carried as a middle name, consistent with the AKA entry 'Minnie Broyles'.

BIRTH DATE CONFLICT: This SS NUMIDENT states 28 February 1877; a Texas death certificate (K36Y-YFJ) for the same individual states 28 October 1877. Both agree on day 28 and year 1877; they differ only in month (February vs October). Minnie self-reported this date in 1939, approximately 62 years after the event — she cannot provide primary information about her own birth (not cognitively aware at birth). The SS application is a derivative of the original SS-5 form; the original form was not examined. The month discrepancy must be resolved by corroborating evidence (original SS-5 form, census ages, other contemporary records).

Marriage record: marriage 'Joseph S. Vandyke married Judie A. Broyles, 1 Sep 1886, Saline Co., MO'. Documents I7's marriage to I3. Supporting assertion.

George+Sidney marriage record: marriage fact 'George Broyles married Miss Sidney Shoemaker, 18 May 1856, Cooper Co., MO'. Directly establishes LCPC-PZK's first marriage to KP3V-5CY and its date. This marriage is the anchor for identifying the half-siblings (children born 1856–1866) vs. full siblings (children born after the second marriage ~1867).

conflicts.description: Minnie Bell Strickland (I4) birth month: SS NUMIDENT (6K7V-X4BQ, a_063) gives '28 Feb 1877'; Texas death certificate (K36Y-YFJ, a_074) gives '28 Oct 1877'. Day 28 and year 1877 agree; only the month differs (February vs. October).
conflicts.independence_analysis: The SS NUMIDENT derives from Minnie's own SS-5 application filed October 1939; the Texas death certificate reflects a family informant's report in November 1971, ~32 years later. The two documents were created independently by different persons for different purposes (benefit enrollment vs. death registration). However, both informants may have drawn on the same family oral tradition about Minnie's birth date, which reduces their independence on this specific point. They are treated as substantially independent — neither is derived from the other — but neither can claim primary-information status for a birth date ~62–94 years earlier.
conflicts.weighing_analysis: Both sources are secondary information about the birth date: Minnie could not have primary knowledge of her own birth (she was a newborn), and the 1971 family informant was not an eyewitness. The SS self-report (Feb) carries the advantage of being Minnie's own statement about herself, but it was made 62 years post-event and could reflect imperfect family oral tradition rather than documentary knowledge. The death-certificate informant (Oct) was further removed: an unnamed family member reporting in 1971, 94 years after the event, with no reason to prefer October over February beyond what they were told or believed. Critically, both the day (28) and year (1877) agree across both sources — a strong indicator that the underlying family tradition is substantially correct and the discrepancy is a single-element transcription error or memory lapse in one source. Without a primary source (birth certificate, church baptism register, or family bible), neither month can be preferred. The 1880 census shows Minnie's age as 3 (implying birth in 1876–1877) — consistent with 1877 but too coarse to resolve the month. The conflict cannot be definitively resolved with available evidence.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: Unresolvable with current evidence. The month discrepancy (February vs. October) cannot be adjudicated on available sources alone. To resolve, the original SS-5 form (not the NUMIDENT summary) should be requested from the Social Security Administration, and the original Texas death certificate image should be examined to confirm the informant's statement. Any family bible, church baptismal record, or Missouri birth registration from 1877 (rare before statehood mandate of 1909) would provide authoritative evidence. The conflict is noted honestly per GPS Element 4 but does not block q_001 — Minnie's identification as a full sibling of Charles Robinson Broyles (b. 9 Dec 1878) rests on her father (George Broyles LCPC-PZK) and mother (Mary Elizabeth Davis LCPC-PDH) as named in multiple concordant sources; the birth month is not material to that conclusion.

conflicts.description: Geo. W. Broyles (I2) maternal parentage: the 1880 census (a_028) asserts he is 'child of Elizabeth Broyles' (= Mary Elizabeth Davis, the 1880 household wife), but the proof classifies him as a child of Sidney Shoemaker (KP3V-5CY), George Broyles's first wife, on the basis of birth chronology (b. ~1861, before the first confirmed Davis child in ~1868).
conflicts.independence_analysis: a_028 is a 1880 census entry recorded approximately 19 years after Geo. W.'s birth (~1861); the informant was likely a household member or George himself. a_025 is the birth-year assertion (~1861) from the same census. The 1860 census (a_084, a_090, a_097) and the 1856 marriage record (a_119) are independent corroborating sources for the Sidney Shoemaker family unit. The conflict is between the census's implied maternal relationship (co-residence with current wife = Elizabeth/Davis) and the chronological inference drawn from cross-record comparison.
conflicts.weighing_analysis: A 19th-century census enumerator recording 'relationship to wife' captured the current household structure, not necessarily biological parentage. The 1880 census routinely listed all children of the head under the current wife even when some were from a prior marriage (stepchildren were not separately coded until later censuses). The chronological evidence is more probative: (1) George and Sidney Shoemaker married 18 May 1856 (a_119); (2) the 1860 census confirms Sidney as wife with children born 1858 and 1859 (a_084, a_090, a_097); (3) Geo. W.'s birth year ~1861 fits naturally as the third Shoemaker child; (4) the first confirmed Davis child is Judy A. (born ~1868 per a_029 area, corroborated by Myrtle's death cert naming Davis as mother), meaning Davis did not enter the picture until ~1867 at the earliest — by which time Geo. W. was already ~6 years old. It is biologically and chronologically impossible for Davis to be Geo. W.'s mother if she married George around 1867 and Geo. W. was born ~1861. The census's 'child of Elizabeth Broyles' reflects co-residence, not biological parentage. The chronological evidence from multiple independent sources is preferred.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: Resolved in favor of the Sidney Shoemaker (KP3V-5CY) maternal classification. The 1880 census assertion (a_028) that Geo. W. is 'child of Elizabeth Broyles' reflects co-residence in the current household, a standard census recording convention that did not distinguish stepchildren from biological children. The convergent chronological evidence from the 1856 marriage record (S8), the 1860 census (S5, showing Sidney's children in 1858-1859 sequence), and Geo. W.'s own birth year of ~1861 — all before the first confirmed Davis child in ~1868 — conclusively places Geo. W. within the Shoemaker marriage period. The conflict does not affect the sibling conclusion: Geo. W. is a confirmed half-sibling of Charles Robinson Broyles through their shared father George Broyles.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Searched FamilySearch federal censuses for Missouri 1860–1880 (Saline and Cooper counties), Missouri and Texas vital records (death certificates), US Social Security NUMIDENT, and Missouri county marriage records 1800–1991. Eight distinct records were located and extracted across nine plan items (one skipped — 1870 census negative after search). No original birth records for Missouri in this period were accessible; all sources are derivative indexes. Research declared exhaustive (q_001 status: exhaustive_declared) with low overturn risk.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Proof Summary: Siblings of Charles Robinson Broyles (MJ7L-SL9)

### Research Question

What children were enumerated in the household of George Broyles (LCPC-PZK, born about 1830 Tennessee, residing Marshall, Saline County, Missouri) in the 1880 federal census, and which of those children are siblings of Charles Robinson Broyles (MJ7L-SL9, born 9 December 1878)?

### Conclusion

Charles Robinson Broyles had at least five siblings through two of his father's marriages. Three were **full siblings** through George Broyles's second wife Mary Elizabeth Davis: **Judy A. Broyles** (born about 1868, later Vandyke), **Minnie Bell Broyles** (born 1877, later Strickland), and **Myrtle Leana Broyles** (born 17 May 1886, later Cartmill). Three were **half-siblings** through George Broyles's first wife Sidney Shoemaker (KP3V-5CY): **William H. Broyles** (born about 1858), **Geo. W. Broyles** (born about 1861), and **Milton M. Broyles** (born about 1859). This conclusion is rated **probable**: all accessed sources are derivative indexes rather than original images, one conflict in Minnie's birth date remains unresolved (c_001), and Milton M. Broyles's identity rests on a single source.

### Evidence and Analysis

#### Anchor Record: 1880 Federal Census

The central record for this conclusion is the 1880 United States Census, which enumerates the household of Geo. Broyles in Marshall, Saline County, Missouri:

> "United States, Census, 1880," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:M6F6-6VT : accessed 2026-07-23), entry for Geo. Broyles household, Marshall, Saline County, Missouri.

The census lists George Broyles as head, wife Elizabeth Broyles (born 1851, Missouri), and five children: Wm. H. Broyles (son, born 1858, Missouri), Geo. W. Broyles (son, born 1861, Missouri), Judy A. Broyles (daughter, born 1868, Missouri), Minnie Broyles (daughter, born 1877, Missouri), and Chas. Broyles (son, born 1879, Missouri). The youngest child, Chas. Broyles (born 1879, Missouri), is identified with Charles Robinson Broyles (MJ7L-SL9, born 9 December 1878) — the convergence of name, birth year, birthplace, and FamilySearch's source attachment to MJ7L-SL9's profile leaves no reasonable doubt. This record directly places Charles in the same household as four siblings and establishes their co-residence as of June 1880.

#### George Broyles's Two Marriages: Establishing the Sibling Classification

The evidence shows George Broyles married twice, producing two sets of children. The earlier marriage is documented in a Missouri marriage record:

> "Missouri, County Marriage, Naturalization, and Court Records, 1800-1991", FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:WGTK-9P6Z : accessed 2026-07-23), entry for George Broyles and Miss Sidney Shoemaker, 18 May 1856.

This record establishes George Broyles's union with Sidney Shoemaker (KP3V-5CY) beginning in 1856. The 1860 federal census documents the first family:

> "United States, Census, 1860," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MHZ5-ZV6 : accessed 2026-07-23), entry for George Broyles household, Palestine Township, Cooper County, Missouri.

The 1860 census shows George Broyles with wife Sidney Broyles (born 1839, Missouri) and two sons: W H Broyles (born 1858, Missouri) and Milton M Broyles (born 1859, Missouri). These children — William H. and Milton M. — are thus confirmed as children of George and his first wife Sidney Shoemaker, making them half-siblings of Charles Robinson Broyles. Geo. W. Broyles (born about 1861) appears in the 1880 census as George's son; his birth year falls within the period of the Shoemaker marriage (1856 through approximately 1866–67, before the children of Mary Elizabeth Davis begin appearing), classifying him as a half-sibling by inference from birth chronology. No record explicitly names Sidney Shoemaker as Geo. W.'s mother; this classification rests on the elimination of Mary Elizabeth Davis as his mother, whose children are born 1868 and later.

By 1880, the household wife is Elizabeth Broyles (born 1851, Missouri), identified from death certificate evidence as Mary Elizabeth Davis. The Davis children — born 1868 and later (Judy A., Minnie, and Charles) — are full siblings of Charles.

#### Corroborating Evidence for the Davis Children

**Myrtle Leana Cartmill (I5):** The Texas death certificate directly names her parentage:

> "Texas, Deaths, 1890-1977," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:KSY2-LWQ : accessed 23 July 2026), entry for Myrtle Leana Cartmill, 16 January 1970; citing Texas Department of Health, Austin.

The certificate records her birth as 17 May 1886 in Marshall, Missouri, father George Broyles, mother Mary Elizabeth Davis. Though secondary evidence (reported at death, 84 years after the fact), this record independently corroborates Myrtle's parentage and identifies the second wife's full name as Mary Elizabeth Davis. Myrtle is not enumerated in the 1880 census (born 1886) but is established as a sibling of Charles based on shared parentage.

**Minnie Bell Strickland (I4):** Two independent records corroborate Minnie's parentage. The Social Security NUMIDENT:

> "United States, Social Security Numerical Identification Files (NUMIDENT), 1936-2007," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:6K7V-X4BQ : accessed 23 July 2026), entry for Minnie Broyles Strickland; NUMIDENT database derived from SS-5 application filed October 1939.

names father George Broyles and mother Elizabeth Davis, and places her birth in Marshall, Saline County, Missouri. The Texas death certificate:

> "Texas, Deaths, 1890-1977", FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:K36Y-YFJ : accessed 2026-07-23), entry for Minnie Bell Strickland and Broyles, 5 November 1971.

gives her birth as 28 October 1877 in Missouri, father's surname Broyles. Both records are consistent with the 1880 census enumeration of Minnie Broyles (born 1877). **A conflict exists:** the NUMIDENT gives the birth date as 28 February 1877 while the death certificate gives 28 October 1877. Both are secondary evidence; neither can be preferred over the other, and this conflict (c_001) remains unresolved. It does not affect the sibling relationship — both records consistently identify Minnie as George Broyles's daughter born in Missouri circa 1877.

The 1896 Saline County marriage record further corroborates Minnie's identity:

> "Missouri, County Marriage, Naturalization, and Court Records, 1800-1991," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:6XWX-JNLS : accessed 23 July 2026), entry for Dee Z Zumwalt and Minnie Broyles, 14 Apr 1896.

**Judy A. Broyles (I3):** The 1886 Saline County marriage record documents Judy's lineage:

> "Missouri, County Marriage, Naturalization, and Court Records, 1800-1991", FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:6XF4-YVCP : accessed 2026-07-23), Entry for Joseph S Vandyke and Judie A Broyles, 1 Sep 1886.

This record names her father as George Broyles, directly corroborating her status as George's daughter and, by extension, as a full sibling of Charles Robinson Broyles.

#### Conflicting Evidence

One conflict was formally identified and entered as c_001: the birth date of Minnie Bell Strickland (I4). The SS NUMIDENT (a_063) gives 28 February 1877; the Texas death certificate (a_074) gives 28 October 1877. Both records are secondary evidence created decades after the birth event. The NUMIDENT was self-reported by Minnie herself in 1939; the death certificate was reported by an informant at death in 1971. Both may draw on the same family oral tradition and neither can be demonstrated to be more reliable. Primary birth records for Missouri circa 1877 were not accessible to resolve the discrepancy. The conflict is limited to the birth month and does not affect the parentage or sibling conclusion.

### Limitations and GPS Assessment

1. **Derivative sources throughout.** All records accessed are FamilySearch derivative indexes. No original images were examined. Index transcription errors cannot be ruled out, though no specific error was detected that would alter the conclusion.

2. **Unresolved birth-date conflict (c_001).** The Minnie birth month conflict between S3 and S4 cannot be resolved with available evidence. This prevents a "proved" GPS rating for the overall question.

3. **Milton M. Broyles (I6) rests on a single source.** The 1860 federal census is the only record locating Milton in the Broyles family. No later record corroborates his continued relationship to the household. His identity as a half-sibling of Charles is probable but not independently verified.

4. **Geo. W. Broyles (I2) maternal classification is inferred.** No record explicitly names Sidney Shoemaker as Geo. W.'s mother. The half-sibling classification rests on birth chronology: his birth circa 1861 falls within the Shoemaker marriage period and before the earliest Davis child (Judy A., born about 1868).

5. **Missouri vital records were not accessible.** Civil registration of births in Missouri began in 1883 but compliance was incomplete through the 1890s; even if birth records existed for the Broyles children of this era, none were found in accessible form. Such records, if located, could provide primary direct evidence of parentage.

### Sources Cited

1. "United States, Census, 1880," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:M6F6-6VT : accessed 2026-07-23), entry for Geo. Broyles household, Marshall, Saline County, Missouri.
2. "Texas, Deaths, 1890-1977," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:KSY2-LWQ : accessed 23 July 2026), entry for Myrtle Leana Cartmill, 16 January 1970; citing Texas Department of Health, Austin.
3. "United States, Social Security Numerical Identification Files (NUMIDENT), 1936-2007," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:6K7V-X4BQ : accessed 23 July 2026), entry for Minnie Broyles Strickland; NUMIDENT database derived from SS-5 application filed October 1939.
4. "Texas, Deaths, 1890-1977", FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:K36Y-YFJ : accessed 2026-07-23), entry for Minnie Bell Strickland and Broyles, 5 November 1971.
5. "United States, Census, 1860," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MHZ5-ZV6 : accessed 2026-07-23), entry for George Broyles household, Palestine Township, Cooper County, Missouri.
6. "Missouri, County Marriage, Naturalization, and Court Records, 1800-1991", FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:6XF4-YVCP : accessed 2026-07-23), Entry for Joseph S Vandyke and Judie A Broyles, 1 Sep 1886.
7. "Missouri, County Marriage, Naturalization, and Court Records, 1800-1991," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:6XWX-JNLS : accessed 23 July 2026), entry for Dee Z Zumwalt and Minnie Broyles, 14 Apr 1896.
8. "Missouri, County Marriage, Naturalization, and Court Records, 1800-1991", FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:WGTK-9P6Z : accessed 2026-07-23), entry for George Broyles and Miss Sidney Shoemaker, 18 May 1856.

quirks: Missouri statewide birth registration began 1909 but was poorly observed until mid-1910s — pre-1909 births rely on church records, family bibles, and census ages.
Missouri statewide death registration began 1883 but was poorly observed early — deaths before ~1895 may only appear in church or cemetery records.
The 1890 federal population census for Saline County was destroyed in the 1921 Commerce Department fire; only the Veterans/Union Widows Schedule survives (imageGroup 004679097, 98% indexed).
Saline County probate and deed records are 0% name-indexed on FamilySearch but are full-text searchable — use fulltext_search with the imageGroupNumber.
Catholic parish records for Marshall, Saline County (imageGroup 008133031, 1871-1954) include baptisms, marriages, and deaths; full-text searchable but 0% name-indexed.
Church records for Malta Bend (imageGroup 008474084, 1860-1899) and Shackleford (imageGroup 008116687, 1852-1954) are browse-only — 0% indexed and not full-text searchable.
Saline County marriage records are well-covered in FS with multiple volumes 1835-1910 at 48-65% indexed and full-text searchable; also searchable via collection 2060668.

1860 Census for Saline County: imageGroup 005170226_003_M9CV-6H8 (98% indexed). 1870: imageGroup 004273849_001_M9CN-M42 (97% indexed). 1900: imageGroups 004118864_002_M92X-W2Q and 004118865_001_M9KY-X4M (95-97% indexed).

## Saline County, Missouri — Locality Guide (1856–1910)

**Standard place:** Saline, Missouri, United States. County seat: Marshall. Formed 1821 from Howard County; no boundary changes in target period.

### Census
- 1860 (98% indexed, imageGroup 005170226_003_M9CV-6H8)
- 1870 (97% indexed, imageGroup 004273849_001_M9CN-M42)
- 1880 (indexed nationally via FS/Ancestry)
- 1890 population census DESTROYED; Veterans Schedule survives (98% indexed, imageGroup 004679097)
- 1900 (95-97% indexed, imageGroups 004118864_002_M92X-W2Q, 004118865_001_M9KY-X4M)

### Vital Records
Birth registration began 1909 (observed from ~mid-1910s). Death registration began 1883 (sparse until ~1895). Pre-registration births/deaths: church records and family bibles.
- FS collections 1680833, 2524491 (births/christenings)
- FS collections 1680837, 2448947 (deaths/burials)
- Missouri State Archives birth/death database: sos.mo.gov

### Marriage
Multiple FS volumes covering 1835-1910, 48-65% indexed + full-text searchable:
- Vol. A 1835-1874 (007513914)
- Vol. D 1875-1881 (007513915)
- Vols. 1-2 1881-1887 (007513916)
- Vols. 3-4 1887-1894 (007513953)
- Vols. 5-6 1894-1902 (007514068)
- Vols. 7-8 1902-1910 (007514069)
Also searchable via collection 2060668 (7.8M records).

### Church Records
- Catholic, Marshall (St. Peter's): imageGroup 008133031 — baptisms/marriages/deaths 1871-1954, full-text searchable, 0% name-indexed
- Shackleford: imageGroup 008116687 — 1852-1954, browse-only
- Malta Bend: imageGroup 008474084 — 1860-1899, browse-only
- Religious birth records Marshall area 1871-1876: imageGroup 007966224, full-text searchable

### Probate
Multiple volumes 1854-1922, all 0% name-indexed but full-text searchable:
- F-G 1854-1866 (007636435), H-I 1866-1871 (007636436), J-K 1871-1876 (007636437)
- L-M 1876-1884 (007630169), N 1884-1886 (007630168)
- Wills A-B 1837-1881 (007630167), C-D 1881-1907 (007630166)
- General index 1821-1891 (007636433)

### Military
- Confederate service records: FS 1932374
- Union service records: FS 1932408
- Confederate pension applications: FS 1865475

### Local History
- Saline County History 1881 (imageGroup 007940688, full-text searchable)
- Missouri Obituaries 1880-1990: FS collection 4373182 (584K records)
- Find A Grave, Missouri Gravestone Photo Project
